chunk_summary: Guidelines for escalating care from outpatient management of moderate acute malnutrition to facility-based care, including referral criteria, pre-referral actions, and handover documentation to ensure continuity of care.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for escalating moderate acute malnutrition cases to facility-based care.
Learn pre-referral actions to stabilize children before transport to a facility.
Recognize the importance of handover documentation in ensuring continuity of care.
Identify community pathways for post-stabilization monitoring and supplementary feeding.
key_facts: Escalation is required if MUAC < 115 mm, WHZ/WLZ < -3, or bilateral pitting oedema appears.
Refer cases with complications like severe dehydration, respiratory distress, or hypothermia to the nearest NRC or paediatric ward implementing FBMC guidelines.
Pre-referral actions include preventing hypoglycaemia, keeping the child warm, cautious dehydration treatment, and administering first-dose antibiotics for severe infections.
Handover documentation should include anthropometry (weight, height/length, MUAC, oedema), appetite status, feeding dysfunction observations, and treatments given.
Post-stabilization care involves supplementary feeding through ICDS entitlements at AWCs and CMAM workflows where available.
reference_quote: Refer to the nearest NRC or paediatric ward implementing FBMC operational guidance for stabilization and nutrition rehabilitation. — Operational guidelines on facility-based management of children with severe acute malnutrition (FBMC)
document_key_topics: Referral criteria for moderate acute malnutrition escalation
